California
Fair Political
Practices Comrnission
June 22, 1990
Jeannine Engl-ish
Executive Director
Little Hoover Commission
1303 J Street, Suite 270
sacramentof cA 9 5814
Your Request for Advice
our File No. A-90-378
Dear Ms. Engl-ish:
This is in response to your request for advice concerning
your duties as executive dj-rector of the Littl-e Hoover commission
under the confl- ict-of-interest 
provisions of the Political 
Reform
Act ('tthe Act'').1/ 
This letter confirms the tel-ephone advice
provided to you on wednesday, June 20, ]-990.
We do not provide advice concerning provisions of law other
than the Act. 
You nay wish to consuft with the Attorney General-'s
office regarding other provisions of l-aw such as section 1o90
which prohibits government officiaLs from having an interest in
contracts with their agencies.
OUESTION
Your spouse i.s under annual contract with the Little 
Hoover
comnission, the governmental agency for which you serve as
executj-ve director. 
His contract is up for renewal. what, if
anything, shoul"d you do with respect to the pendinqr contract
renewal process?
CONCLUS ION
The renewal of your spouse's contract is a governmental
decision whj.ch wil-I affect your economic interests' 
Therefore'
you may not partj-cipate in the renewal of the contract or use your
otfl"ilf 
posltion to influence thj.s governmental decision'
L/ Government code Sections 81ooo-91015. A11 statutory
references are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated.
commission regutations appear at 2 california code of Regulations
Section 18ooo, et seq. A11 references to regulations are to
Title 2, Divi-sion 6 of the California code of Regulations'
428 J Street, Suite 8OO o P.O. Box 8O? . sacramento cA 958O4-O8O7 . (916)322.5660


Fi■e No. A-90-378
Page 2
FACTS
The Lj,ttle lloover Commission (the "commission") has retained
private legaI counsel, Howard L. Dickstein, to serve as its
general counsel. The conmission has entered into annual, contracts
witfr ur. Dickstein's law firm, Dickstein & Merin, for this purpose
since 1986. Sometime after the Iaw firm's last contract lras
renewed by the Commission, you and ur. Dickstein married.
According to the information provided in your letter, the
Commission itself selects the generaf counsel and approves his
fixed price contracts on an annual basis, a process in which you
do not participate. 
fn our telephone conversation of June 15,
1990 you provided the following additional information on this
surjelt. 
The connission considers the terms of the contract lrith
oici<stein & Merin, a partnership, in private session. The
executive director has no direct authority to enter into a
contractual agreement on behalf of the Cornmission for the purpose
of selecting or retaining its private legal counsel .
The onl-y activity carried out by your staff in connection
with the conlract is to furnish the Commission with terrns of the
previous contracts relating to the duties and scope of
iesponsibilities of the legal counsel and the amount of
renumeration paid for his services. Consideratj-on of the renewal
of 1egal counsel is placed automatically on the Cornrnission's
agendi in the month of June of each cafendar year. Support staff
types the new contract.
ANALYSI S
Section 87100 sets forth the general rule concerning
potential conflicts of interest confronting public officials- 
It
ltates that no public official 
at any 1eve1 of state or loca1
governrnent sha11 nake, participate in making or in any lray attempt
io use his or her official position to influence a governmentaL
decision in which the official 
knows or has reason to know he or
she has a financial interest.
An official 
has a financial interest in a decision if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public
generally, on the officiat or a menber of his inmediate family or
on:
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect investment srorth
$1,0o0 or nore.
= 
★ =


File No. A-90-378
Page 3
(c) Any source of incone, other than gifts
and other than toans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terrns available to the public without regard to
official 
status, aggregating $25o or more in val,ue
provj,ded to, received by or promised to the public
official within 12 rnonths prior to the time '$rhen
the decision is rnade.
***
For purposes of Section 87103, indirect
investruent or interest means any investment or
interest owned by the sPouse or dependent child of
a public official, 
by an agent on behalf of a
public official, 
or by a business entity or trust
in which the official, 
the official's 
agents,
spouse, and dependent children own directly,
indirectly, or beneficially a 1o-percent interest
or greater.
section 87103 (a) and (c).
As executive director of the Little Hoover Commj'ssion, you
are a public official. 
(Section a2o4a; Regulation 18700(a) r coPY
enclosed.) Your spouse has an interest in a law partnership,
Dickstein & Merin, which is presumabl-y worth more than 91,000.
Under Section 87103 you have an indirect interest in your spouse's
1aw firm. 
Accordingty, you must disgualify yourself from
participating in any decision which will- have a reasonably
ioreseeable material financial effect on the law partnership which
is distinguishable frorn the effect on the public generally.
In the situation you describe, your spouse is directly
involved in the decision before the Cornmission which will have
sorne financial effect on his 1aw partnership. Under our
regulations, such a financial effect is deerned to be material.
(see Regulation 18702.1(a) (2) r coPY enclosed.)
In your letter to the Commission and in our subsequent
telephone conversation, you stated that you-do not participate in
any manner in the contract renewal process'z/ The only activities
performed by your staff in connection rrith the contract in
question appear to be clerical in nature. Making or participating
in the making of a governmental decision does not j.nclude actions
of pubJ.ic officials 
whj.ch are so1e1y ministerial, secretarial,
zt 
You may not participate in any governmental decision, not iust
the contract renevral- process, that nay have a material financial-
effect on any econornic interest specified in Section 877o3,
including, but not linited to, the Iaw partnership. 
See our
brochure entitled California's Conflict of Interest Law for Public
officials, 
copy enclosed.


File No. A-90-378
Page 4
manual or cl-erical in nature. (Regulation 18700.1(d); cano Advice
Letter No. I-90-19O; Flandrick Advice Letter No- A-85-145, copies
enclosed ) .
Hohrever, please note that I'participate'r in the rnaking of a
governmental decision is interpreted very broadly by the Fair
iolitical 
Practices commission. Regulation 18700, subsection (b)
provides that a public official- rrnakes a governmental decisiontt
irhen he or she (1) votes on a matter; (2) aPPoints a person; (3)
obligates or cornmits his or her agency to any course of action;
or (i) enters into any contractuaL agreement on behalf of his or
her'agency. Subsection (c) of Regulation l'8700 (c) provides that a
publi- ofiicial 
"participates in the rnaking of a governmental
-clecision" when he or she (1) negotiates, without significant
substantive review, with a governmental entity or private person
regarding the decision; ot (2) advises or makes recornmendations to
tfre aecj.lion-maker, either directly or without significant
intervening substantive review. (Regul'ation 187oo; Combs Advice
Letter No. A-89-177; ltcHuqh Advice Letter No. I-89-443, copies
enclosed . )
This means that you may not take any action to approve,
conditionally approve, or disapprove, the renewal- of the contract
or to advise or make reconmendations concerning any term or
provision of the renewal contract.
Furthermore, please note that 'rusing official 
position to
inf luencet' a governmental decision also is interpreted very
broadly by the Fair Politica1 Practices Conmission, in Regufation
18700.1(a), as follo$rs 3
with regard to a governmentaL decisi-on which
is within or before an official's 
agency or an
agency appointed by or subject to the budgetary
control oi nis or her agency, the official 
is
attenpting to use his or her officiaL position to
influence the decision if, for the purpose of
influencing the decision, the official 
contacts, or
appears beiore, or otherwise attempts to influence,
any mernber, officer, 
emptoyee or consultant of the
agency. Attenpts to influence include, but are not
f irnitea to, appearances or contacts by the officiaf
on behalf of a business entity, client, or
customer.
Regulation 18700.1(a) coPy encl'osed-
See McHuqh Advice Letter, supra.
This means that you may not communi-cate with any of the
members of the Cornnission or your staff with respect to the
contract with Dickstein & l{erin.


・          Fi■e No. A-90-378
Page 5
I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any additional questions regarding this
matter, please contact ne at (916) 322-590]-.
sincerely,
scott Hallabrin
Acting General counsel
